channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Hatsune Miku Cosplayer Goes Viral For Selling ‘Feet Juice’ Outside Anime Convention
item.content:encoded:
Thirsty? How would you like some unidentified red juice delivered via a person’s foot? That’ll be $15, please.

This is the deal being offered, report Spanish-language Fuji News on X, by two cosplayers at a U.S. convention. It appears to have taken place at an unofficial Park Con event as part of FanimeCon, which took place over the weekend in San Jose. The big annual anime-themed convention has been running since 1994, and attracts around 30,000 people.

Yeah America isn’t real https://t.co/gdtfIU3sWI pic.twitter.com/RFgZkCztAu

— Jeyjey₊ ⊹₍ᐢᐢ₎ ᰔᩚ (@pantsu_waifu) May 26, 2026

At an outdoor location, two cosplayers were photographed holding signs that read, “FRESH FEET JUICE,” while sitting above coolers containing both a mysterious red liquid and their feet. For $5 people could have a “swig,” and reportedly for $10 to $15 you could instead just lick the juice directly from their feet.

The photos and videos show that they received at least a few willing customers, and that at least one passerby was not enticed: check out this dude’s reaction.

We’re currently in the middle of an unprecedented May heat wave here in the UK, with the hottest-ever temperatures being recorded for this time of year, and right now I’m writing this with an air conditioner offering a futile fight against the heat in my office. And you know, even now this still wouldn’t be my first choice of a way to get a drink. But kudos to the pair for making an easy buck from those more inclined.



https://kotaku.com/cosplay-fanimecon-fresh-feet-juice-anime-convention-2000699493
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Pokémon Crime Spree Escalates To Man Using Chainsaw To Steal $12,000 Worth Of Cards
item.content:encoded:
We’ve been reporting for years on the growing spate of Pokémon card thefts, as the market for the shiny cardboard has ballooned through supply shortages and a scalping free-for-all. But in all the many occasions, we’ve never seen someone complete such a theft using a chainsaw. That’s the allegation (via IGN) against Clayton Warren, arrested in Palm Beach, Florida, and charged with burglary and grand theft, after $12,000 worth of Pokémon cards were stolen from the Collection Realm store in Lake Park.

According to local news source WPTV, the owner of Collection Realm contacted the Palm Beach County Sheriff’s Office toward the end of last week to report that his store had been burgled overnight. Surveillance footage showed someone cutting a triangle-shaped hole in a hurricane-proof window with a battery-powered chainsaw, because Florida. The thief seemingly managed to injure himself in the process, and left blood at the scene. Oh, and he also got his license plate recorded on camera. Via the arrest report we dug up, Warren was caught and arrested later the same day, before being released on a $20,000 surety bond the following evening, May 22. He’s charged with causing damages valued at over $1,000 and theft of property worth $10-$20,000.

WPTV says the store owner watched the surveillance footage and estimated that $12,000 worth of his Pokémon stock was taken in the burglary, including sleeved cards and a bunch of bulk. He also found footage of someone matching Warren’s description visiting the store a couple of days earlier. All in all, the thief sounds like a real pro.

Far be it from me to give advice to would-be burglars, but a few things to generally avoid are loud, cumbersome electric tools, driving your own car right up to the store, and then just cutting yourself open in the process.



https://kotaku.com/chainsaw-burglary-pokemon-cards-store-florida-arrest-2000699484
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
